evidence: `public/sdk.js` fetches `/v1/registry/export` before falling back to cached localStorage; `public/sw.js` stale-revalidates the same export.
why_it_matters: The product describes cache-first offline behavior while shipping network-first with fallback.
next_action: Rewrite to cached fallback or implement a cache-first offline mode with explicit sync.

claim_or_finding: The `/device` PWA cache and manifest do not prove an installable offline app.
evidence: The precache omits `/recipe-worker.js` and `/brand-refresh.css`; the linked manifest starts at `/`, not `/device`; offline-ready UI only checks service-worker registration.
why_it_matters: Installed users can open the wrong app shell or lack required runtime assets offline.
next_action: Create a device manifest, precache all dependencies, and test offline reload.

claim_or_finding: Browser registry and run proof is hash-only or unsigned, not signed receipt evidence.
evidence: Live registry export has source hashes but no signature fields; browser SDK run receipts are local unsigned metadata and server verification is a network call.
why_it_matters: Browser proof language can overstate auditability and offline verification.
next_action: Add signed registry envelopes and signed browser run receipts, or narrow wording.

claim_or_finding: Browser SDK fallback can compile public registry source on the main page.
evidence: `public/sdk.js` intends worker compilation but falls back to `new Function('return (' + r.meta.source + ');')()` when no worker is available.
why_it_matters: A no-Worker path can bypass the stated worker isolation boundary.
next_action: Disable main-thread fallback for public rows or require explicit unsafe mode.

claim_or_finding: On-device healthcare/security/whitepaper copy is broader than current browser/native runtime proof.
evidence: Security, HIPAA, and whitepaper pages describe offline/on-device/zero-egress target behavior while current browser assets fail and native model/adapter/index bridges remain caveated.
why_it_matters: Regulated buyers need proof links, not architecture aspirations, for local-data claims.
next_action: Tie every on-device claim to a passing artifact/browser proof or mark it as target architecture.

claim_or_finding: The documented `@kolm/cli` npm install points to a missing package.
evidence: Live `/docs` shows `npm i -g @kolm/cli`; `npm view @kolm/cli version --json` returned 404.
why_it_matters: The first public install command can fail for new users.
next_action: Publish the package or make GitHub-source install the only documented preview path.

claim_or_finding: The shipped GitHub Action calls root CLI commands that do not exist.
evidence: The action runs `kolm whoami` and `kolm verify`; local CLI probes return unknown command for both; compile help does not document action-required JSON output.
why_it_matters: Copied CI workflows can fail after installation.
next_action: Update the action to current CLI verbs and add action smoke tests.

claim_or_finding: Package-manager shipped labels are ahead of real taps/manifests.
evidence: Live integrations mark Homebrew and Windows package-manager paths shipped; local stubs say preview; checked Homebrew tap, winget path, and Scoop manifest returned 404.
why_it_matters: Users can copy install paths that cannot resolve.
next_action: Render shipped/preview labels from package-manager existence checks.

claim_or_finding: The intended Python package name collides with an unrelated PyPI project.
evidence: `pip index versions kolm` showed versions 1.1.4 and 1.1.3; PyPI JSON identifies it as a Korean LM toolkit by another author, while local `pyproject.toml` also uses `kolm`.
why_it_matters: Kolm cannot assume clean PyPI ownership of that name.
next_action: Choose a new PyPI name or resolve ownership before publishing docs.

claim_or_finding: Python SDK wrappers are syntactically valid but out of contract with the root CLI.
evidence: Python `compile()` uses unsupported flags, `run()` calls `--in --json`, and `verify()` calls missing `kolm verify`.
why_it_matters: The SDK can import but still fail in core workflows.
next_action: Align wrappers to current `compile`, `run`, `inspect`, and `eval` contracts and test them.

claim_or_finding: The root GitHub-source package is not release-curated.
evidence: Root `npm pack --dry-run` produced 390 entries and about 8.6 MB packed, including research docs, tests, public assets, fixture artifacts, workflows, and scripts, with no root license file.
why_it_matters: The preview install path can expose broad repo contents and lacks package hygiene.
next_action: Add `files` or `.npmignore`, include a license, and snapshot package contents in CI.

claim_or_finding: SLSA, Sigstore, and SBOM remain target architecture without release workflows.
evidence: Security copy caveats these as not yet emitted; source search found no id-token, SLSA, Cosign, CycloneDX, publish, or release workflow.
why_it_matters: Supply-chain proof cannot be verified by buyers yet.
next_action: Build a tagged release workflow that emits provenance, signatures, checksums, and SBOMs.

claim_or_finding: Node SDK is labeled shipped publicly but is not published on npm.
evidence: Live integrations label Node/TypeScript shipped; `npm view @kolmogorov/kolm-sdk` returned 404; the local README says the package is not published yet.
why_it_matters: Developers can expect a package that only exists as source.
next_action: Change public label to source preview or publish and smoke the package.

claim_or_finding: The VS Code extension is package-shaped but still carries legacy API/import paths.
evidence: `sdk/vscode/extension.js` passes syntax and package dry-run is small, but it uses a legacy default API host and replacement import text for a legacy scoped SDK package.
why_it_matters: Marketplace publication would ship stale integration guidance.
next_action: Update host/import naming and add VSIX smoke before release.

claim_or_finding: Docker is labeled shipped without evidence of a Kolm release image.
evidence: Live integrations mark Docker shipped, but the example uses upstream Node plus GitHub-source npm install; the repo Dockerfile is a server image and no Docker publish workflow was found.
why_it_matters: Docker users get an example command, not a signed published image.
next_action: Label as Docker recipe or publish a signed CLI/server image.

claim_or_finding: The public cookbook's all-compile claim is not backed by matching specs, gold sets, artifacts, or receipts.
evidence: Live and local `/cookbook` say 30 recipes all compile; repo evidence shows four verified fixture artifacts and no cookbook `.toml` or JSONL proof files.
why_it_matters: The strongest buyer proof surface can overstate product readiness.
next_action: Create a proof manifest and relabel pages as verified, seed, preview, or target.

claim_or_finding: All cookbook compile pages use a flag the current CLI does not parse.
evidence: All 33 cookbook detail pages use `--output`; `cli/kolm.js` reads `--out` for compile output paths.
why_it_matters: Developers copying cookbook commands can fail or write to unexpected defaults.
next_action: Add a tested alias or regenerate snippets from current CLI help.

claim_or_finding: Cookbook run snippets include input forms that are unsupported or misparsed.
evidence: Pages use `--input-file` and `--input-stdin`; a local probe showed `--input` is treated as positional input, producing the wrong sample result.
why_it_matters: Examples can appear to work while running against the wrong input.
next_action: Implement input flags or rewrite all run snippets to positional input plus tests.

claim_or_finding: Cookbook K-score values use a different scale from real fixture artifacts.
evidence: Cookbook pages show roughly 0.81-0.94; `kolm score` on verified fixtures reports composites in the hundreds.
why_it_matters: K-score proof cannot be compared across public pages and CLI output.
next_action: Choose one schema, migrate manifests, and render public values from benchmark artifacts.

claim_or_finding: The four public fixture artifacts are real and should become the canonical proof baseline.
evidence: `tests/artifact-end-to-end.test.js` verifies signature load, embedded evals, benchmark zero egress, tenant params, audit callback, input cap, and tamper rejection for four fixtures.
why_it_matters: The product has credible small proof, but it is diluted by unsupported broader cookbook claims.
next_action: Promote four fixtures as verified and extend that standard before expanding cookbook labels.

claim_or_finding: Cookbook JSON-LD advertises executable five-minute HowTo flows for missing files.
evidence: Detail pages embed install, compile, and run steps for `.toml` and `.kolm` files that were not found in the repo.
why_it_matters: Search-visible metadata can keep stale workflows alive after visible copy changes.
next_action: Validate JSON-LD commands and file references in CI or emit HowTo only for verified recipes.

claim_or_finding: Cookbook latency, size, and compile-time numbers are handwritten without per-page benchmark reports.
evidence: Pages show MB/GB artifact sizes, minute compile times, and device latency tables; only four tiny fixture artifacts have CI benchmark assertions.
why_it_matters: Performance claims lack reproducible provenance.
next_action: Store benchmark reports and render metrics from them.

claim_or_finding: The cookbook has 33 detail pages but only 30 index links.
evidence: Local inventory found unlinked pages for drug-name redaction, intake triage, and tactical-edge intelligence.
why_it_matters: Recipe count and navigation can drift silently.
next_action: Generate the cookbook index from the same manifest as page generation.

claim_or_finding: Regulated cookbook recipes imply domain gates without regulated proof bundles.
evidence: Healthcare, finance, legal, edge, and web3 pages describe domain-specific datasets, verifiers, and offline behavior, but no matching corpora, artifacts, receipts, or benchmark files were found.
why_it_matters: Regulated buyers may treat illustrative pages as validated compliance evidence.
next_action: Mark regulated recipes as illustrative until proof bundles exist.

claim_or_finding: The legacy demo runbook is stale relative to current product proof.
evidence: `DEMO.md` still carries old deployment, brand, package, and benchmark framing while current verified proof is artifact-fixture based.
why_it_matters: Sales prep can revive claims already superseded by the current Kolm positioning.
next_action: Archive or rewrite the demo around current domain, current install path, and verified artifacts only.

claim_or_finding: `kolm.yaml` is described as a schema-governed project contract, but runtime use is hand-parsed and permissive.
evidence: The live schema requires fields and forbids extras; `src/project.js` defaults missing values and ignores unknown or malformed lines.
why_it_matters: A config can be invalid by schema but still affect runtime behavior.
next_action: Add schema validation and use one parser across CLI, MCP, hooks, and sidecars.

claim_or_finding: Hook policy can fail open unless a script exits exactly 2.
evidence: `src/hooks.js` blocks only on exit code 2; other non-zero exits and timeout 124 continue as advisory warnings.
why_it_matters: PreRun redaction or PreCompile policy hooks can fail while the operation proceeds.
next_action: Define blocking/advisory modes and make policy Pre* hooks fail closed by default.

claim_or_finding: The schema promises hook stdout event mutation that runtime ignores.
evidence: Live schema describes `additionalContext` and `updatedInput`; `src/hooks.js` captures stdout only for display and does not parse or apply changes.
why_it_matters: Redaction or context-enrichment hooks cannot work as documented.
next_action: Implement a mutation schema with tests or remove the claim.

claim_or_finding: Project hooks are arbitrary local shell execution and need explicit trust UX.
evidence: Runtime runs hook strings through `cmd /c` or `/bin/sh -c` from the discovered project root.
why_it_matters: Opening an untrusted repo and running Kolm can execute project-provided commands.
next_action: Add hook inventory, trust warnings, and a no-hooks flag before launch.

claim_or_finding: Project `k_min` gates do not match the current K-score scale.
evidence: Schema caps `k_min` at 1 and init comments show 0.85, while real fixture composites are in the hundreds and MCP compares raw composite values.
why_it_matters: The documented gate is effectively meaningless for current artifacts.
next_action: Normalize or migrate K-score before applying MCP gates.

claim_or_finding: Project schema allows SSE and MCP host/port config that serve does not apply.
evidence: `cmdServe` uses CLI flags for `--http` and `--port`; server supports stdio and HTTP JSON-RPC, not SSE; project `mcp` config is parsed but not used by serve flags.
why_it_matters: Users can configure transports that do not run.
next_action: Remove unsupported transports or wire project config into serve with tests.

claim_or_finding: `allowed-tools` is parsed but not enforced in generated sidecars or MCP responses.
evidence: Project parser stores allowed tools, MCP strips internal metadata before wire output, and sidecars hardcode `allowed-tools: []`.
why_it_matters: The config implies sub-agent permission governance that does not exist.
next_action: Enforce allowed tools end to end or remove the field from launch docs.

claim_or_finding: `kolm doctor` is presented as wiring verification but checks only coarse environment state.
evidence: Doctor checks config, API key, cloud health, receipt secret, Node, Docker, optional key, project config, and artifact counts; it does not smoke MCP clients, harness configs, hooks, sidecars, or `k_min`.
why_it_matters: Developers can get a passing doctor while agent wiring is broken.
next_action: Add harness/MCP/hook/sidecar checks or narrow doctor copy.

claim_or_finding: Generated SKILL sidecars overstate runtime egress enforcement.
evidence: `writeSkillSidecar` says runtime egress is patched at the process boundary; egress patching is in the benchmark harness, not normal run or MCP calls.
why_it_matters: Agent-facing proof text can claim stronger isolation than runtime provides.
next_action: Rewrite sidecar guarantee or move egress monitoring into runtime.

claim_or_finding: There are no focused tests for project config and hook governance.
evidence: Search of `tests/` found no parser, hook execution, bypass, init, doctor, sidecar, or project-MCP tests.
why_it_matters: A powerful local automation surface can drift without CI signal.
next_action: Add a project automation CI gate covering config, hooks, doctor, sidecars, and MCP config.

claim_or_finding: The evolve surface still overstates closed-loop local model improvement.
evidence: Live and local evolve metadata and hero copy describe local fine-tune, local lookup, K-score promotion, and no phone-home behavior, while body caveats say adapter-driven improvement is target architecture.
why_it_matters: First-screen and search-visible claims can imply a shipped learning loop that is not implemented yet.
next_action: Narrow evolve metadata, hero, terminal samples, and status labels to preview scope.

claim_or_finding: `tune eval` cannot currently load embedded eval cases through the inspect path.
evidence: `evalRevision` reads `info.evals || []`; `inspectArtifact` returns `evals_n` only; a local sample-artifact probe returned `pass: 0/0` and K-score `0.4415`.
why_it_matters: Promotion gates can reflect plumbing failure rather than artifact or adapter quality.
next_action: Load eval cases from the artifact bundle or reuse a revision-aware `evalArtifact` path.

claim_or_finding: Tune scoring ignores the candidate adapter revision.
evidence: `evalRevision` accepts `revision` but never loads files under `revisions/<rev>`; current artifact runs execute deterministic recipes without adapter input.
why_it_matters: A promoted revision may not change runtime behavior for current artifacts.
next_action: Implement adapter-backed eval or relabel tune eval as artifact re-validation.

claim_or_finding: Tune promotion is a local HEAD flip, not a signed artifact promotion.
evidence: `promoteRevision` does not enforce `require_improvement`; it writes `HEAD` and `head.prev` but does not re-sign the `.kolm`, update the manifest, or hot-reload serve.
why_it_matters: Users can believe a portable artifact improved when only local side state changed.
next_action: Define promotion semantics and enforce improvement, signing, and reload behavior if those claims remain.

claim_or_finding: Local tune captures raw production inputs and outputs without governance controls.
evidence: `appendCapture` writes raw `input`, `output`, `recipe`, and `latency_us` into `captures.jsonl` with no redaction, encryption, retention, consent, or purge metadata.
why_it_matters: Production training signal can contain sensitive data with no lifecycle policy.
next_action: Add a local capture governance schema before enterprise or regulated positioning.

claim_or_finding: Tune airgap is an explicit mode, not a global no-phone-home guarantee.
evidence: `--airgap` sets offline env flags and rejects missing/local-invalid base paths, but non-airgap training can use normal model loading and there is no OS-level network denial.
why_it_matters: Broad offline claims can be false depending on flags and Python behavior.
next_action: Make no-network claims conditional on `--airgap` and add process-level network denial tests.

claim_or_finding: `tune watch` can automate a flawed promotion path.
evidence: `watchAndEvolve` triggers step, eval, and promote from capture-count deltas, ignores `sample_size`, and has no lock or backoff.
why_it_matters: Autonomous tuning can amplify incorrect eval and promotion semantics.
next_action: Disable auto-promote or add locking, sample policy, fixed eval, and manual approval before launch.

claim_or_finding: Tune troubleshooting contracts drift from CLI behavior.
evidence: `docs/TUNE.md` says missing Python deps exit 64, but `cmdTune` exits 1 for all step failures; the missing-trainer error mentions `KOLM_TUNE_TRAINER`, which code does not read.
why_it_matters: Automation and users cannot reliably diagnose tune setup failures.
next_action: Preserve trainer exit codes and implement or remove the trainer override hint.

claim_or_finding: There are no focused tests for local tune and evolution governance.
evidence: Search of `tests/` found no tune, capture, promote, rollback, watch, airgap, trainer, adapter, or evolve-copy coverage.
why_it_matters: A high-risk public feature can drift without CI signal.
next_action: Add a deterministic tune fixture and release gate for tune subcommands and claims.

claim_or_finding: Local RAG query exists, but artifact runtime injection is still not shipped.
evidence: `src/rag.js` implements index/query/attach sidecars; `artifact-runner` and `verifier` do not inject `lib.rag` into recipes, and the evolve page labels runtime injection target architecture.
why_it_matters: RAG attach can be mistaken for signed runtime-grounded artifact behavior.
next_action: Keep RAG runtime claims caveated or wire sidecars into signed runner behavior.

claim_or_finding: The current local homepage reintroduced a forbidden regulated-healthcare privacy phrase.
evidence: Root `npm test` now fails `tests/site.test.js` because `public/index.html` contains `PHI never leaves`; the same test still also has the known production-like readiness failure.
why_it_matters: The claim gate is catching exactly the kind of local-data guarantee that earlier research flagged as unsafe without deployment-bound proof.
next_action: Clean up the homepage copy or tie it to explicit customer-controlled deployment proof before release.

claim_or_finding: The focused site claim gate is red on the local homepage.
evidence: `node --test .\tests\site.test.js` fails because `public/index.html` contains the forbidden regulated-healthcare phrase `PHI never leaves`.
why_it_matters: This should block deployment until the homepage is scoped to proven customer-controlled deployment behavior.
next_action: Rewrite or remove the healthcare card before release.

claim_or_finding: Local homepage metadata and FAQ revive model-bearing artifact claims.
evidence: Meta/social descriptions and FAQ text say the artifact contains a model and runs on phone-like targets, while `src/artifact.js` says current model content is pointer metadata and LoRA weights are future.
why_it_matters: Search snippets and FAQs can overclaim before users read implementation caveats.
next_action: Rewrite metadata and FAQ around recipe-tier artifacts and current fixture proof.

claim_or_finding: Local structured data embeds high-risk runtime and compliance claims.
evidence: JSON-LD lists on-device zero-egress behavior, optional Sigstore co-signature, BAA availability, and optional VPC peering.
why_it_matters: Machine-readable claims can become SEO and procurement evidence without visual review.
next_action: Add structured-data claim linting or remove unproved fields.

claim_or_finding: The live homepage still presents older model-artifact positioning.
evidence: Live `https://kolm.ai/` says Kolm compiles data into a model, returns an offline artifact, shows a multi-GB artifact, and implies model/LoRA/phone capabilities.
why_it_matters: Public visitors see claims that local research already narrowed as unproved for v0.
next_action: Replace live homepage after local claim cleanup or add explicit preview labels.

claim_or_finding: The live docs remain on the older package and hosted API surface.
evidence: Live `https://kolm.ai/docs` still says 21 endpoints and uses old install, host, SDK, MCP, and brand framing.
why_it_matters: Developers can copy commands that do not match the current codebase or package plan.
next_action: Deploy generated docs from current route/package manifests.

claim_or_finding: The homepage claim gate is too exact-string dependent.
evidence: The gate caught `PHI never leaves` but not equivalent variants like data never moves, phone inference, zero network, VPC boundary, on-device zero egress, or strictly better compiles.
why_it_matters: Unsafe claims can bypass the test by changing wording.
next_action: Add phrase-family checks and a structured claim manifest.

claim_or_finding: Homepage demos should be generated from verified fixtures, not handwritten model stories.
evidence: Current homepage demos use patient-intake, model specialization, phone/VPC host labels, and K-score numbers; verified fixture proof exists for sample, redactor, extractor, and classifier artifacts.
why_it_matters: Handwritten demos can drift beyond what tests prove.
next_action: Render homepage proof blocks from fixture artifacts and benchmark reports.

claim_or_finding: README and package metadata still conflict with their own implementation caveats.
evidence: README/package top copy says signed artifacts run locally with model-like framing, while later README notes v0 artifacts are pointers and recipe/eval/receipt data.
why_it_matters: Top-level metadata can be treated as the contract before caveats are read.
next_action: Align README and package descriptions with the implementation note.

claim_or_finding: Local tests alone do not prove live claim safety.
evidence: Live home and docs still expose older claims while local files contain a different set of homepage claims.
why_it_matters: A green local build could still leave unsafe public pages online.
next_action: Add a live forbidden-claim scan before and after deployment.

claim_or_finding: Homepage regulated-industry cards need shipped/manual/planned labels.
evidence: Healthcare, legal, finance, and device cards imply current regulated data, privileged data, model-risk, and phone/offline behavior without proof bundles on the homepage.
why_it_matters: Regulated buyers may treat illustrative cards as implementation guarantees.
next_action: Mark regulated cards as target patterns or link each to proof and owner-reviewed limits.

claim_or_finding: Current server/local JS execution is not a production sandbox for untrusted recipes.
evidence: `src/verifier.js` uses `node:vm`, a string denylist, and cooperative timeout; official Node docs say VM is not a security mechanism for untrusted code.
why_it_matters: Public or customer-supplied JavaScript can become a code-execution and availability risk if treated as sandboxed.
next_action: Restrict current JS runtime to trusted/curated recipes and add trust levels.

claim_or_finding: Recipe timeouts are cooperative and cannot stop a non-returning loop.
evidence: `runWithTimeout` calls the recipe first and checks elapsed time after return; there is no killable worker/process boundary in the Node path.
why_it_matters: A malicious or buggy recipe can hang the main runtime process.
next_action: Move untrusted execution into a killable worker, process, WASM runtime, or external sandbox.

claim_or_finding: Public visibility is being used where a trust policy is needed.
evidence: Concepts have `visibility` but no reviewed/curated/untrusted trust level; `/v1/public/run` and registry export can expose executable public source.
why_it_matters: Public registry source can be treated as safe without review metadata.
next_action: Add trust tier, review state, publisher identity, source envelope, and revocation epoch before public execution/export.

claim_or_finding: Benchmark zero-egress proof is not runtime-wide egress enforcement.
evidence: `src/benchmark.js` patches network APIs only during `benchmarkArtifact`, then restores them; normal run, hosted runtime, MCP, and browser paths do not install the monitor.
why_it_matters: Marketing or sidecar copy can overstate benchmark evidence as process-boundary enforcement.
next_action: Move egress policy into runtime or narrow copy to benchmark-scoped proof.

claim_or_finding: Browser sandbox assets currently fail syntax checks.
evidence: `node --check public/sdk.js` and `node --check public/recipe-worker.js` both fail on corrupted ternary expressions.
why_it_matters: The browser worker sandbox cannot be used as proof while the shipped assets do not parse.
next_action: Add external asset syntax checks and fix SDK/worker generation.

claim_or_finding: The browser SDK retains a main-thread unsafe fallback.
evidence: `public/sdk.js` can compile registry source with `new Function` when worker execution is unavailable or unsafe mode is used.
why_it_matters: Public registry code can run in page context in some runtimes.
next_action: Disable main-thread fallback by default and require explicit unsafe opt-in.

claim_or_finding: Sandbox tests cover fixtures but not malicious recipes.
evidence: Artifact tests verify signatures, evals, benchmark egress, input cap, audit callback, and tamper rejection, but not denylist primitives, infinite loops, memory pressure, async tasks, or browser globals.
why_it_matters: The sandbox posture can regress without CI signal.
next_action: Add verifier, public-run, browser-worker, and malicious-fixture tests.

claim_or_finding: Hosted registry runtime lacks the artifact runner's input cap and trust partition.
evidence: `runtime.runVersion` executes registry source through `compileJs` and caches by version id; the 1 MiB input cap lives in `runArtifact`, not this path.
why_it_matters: Large inputs or revoked/untrusted versions can stress or persist in runtime paths.
next_action: Add shared input caps plus trust/revocation-aware compiled cache keys.

claim_or_finding: WASM/Wasmtime remains the best-aligned target but is not productized.
evidence: `compileWasm` is a minimal stub with no fixtures, limits, WASI policy, or conformance tests; official Wasmtime/WebAssembly docs describe stronger sandbox primitives.
why_it_matters: The strategic sandbox target is still separate from shipped JS runtime behavior.
next_action: Prototype a WASM/DSL fixture before public-untrusted recipe claims.

claim_or_finding: Local artifact audit previews can leak sensitive input snippets if persisted.
evidence: `runArtifact` audit rows include `input_preview` up to 200 characters.
why_it_matters: Regulated deployments need data-minimized logs and redaction modes.
next_action: Add hash-only or redacted audit modes before regulated audit-log claims.

claim_or_finding: Live docs still expose the older API and package surface.
evidence: Live `https://kolm.ai/docs` presents old SDK package snippets, old hosted API calls, old MCP package wording, and old brand footer.
why_it_matters: The public docs can send users to commands and hosts that do not match the current Kolm product.
next_action: Deploy current docs with evidence-backed labels or mark the live page legacy.

claim_or_finding: The documented public CLI npm package is still missing.
evidence: Local docs show `npm i -g @kolm/cli`, while `npm view` for that package returned 404.
why_it_matters: The first install path can fail before a user reaches the product.
next_action: Publish and smoke the package or make GitHub-source preview the only install command.

claim_or_finding: The current source package is an oversized worktree snapshot.
evidence: Escalated `npm pack --dry-run --json` reported 699 entries, 243697965 bytes packed, 272 `tmp/` entries, 62 research-doc entries, no package lock, and no root license.
why_it_matters: The preview install can ship screenshots, internal research, public-site bulk, and weak release metadata.
next_action: Add a root package allowlist and dry-run CI budget before recommending source installs broadly.

claim_or_finding: The shipped GitHub Action contract does not match the root CLI.
evidence: The action calls `whoami`, `verify`, and `compile --json`; local probes show the first two commands are unknown and compile help lacks JSON output.
why_it_matters: Users copying the action can fail even when source install succeeds.
next_action: Rewrite the action around current commands or implement and test the missing contract.

claim_or_finding: The Python release path has a package-name collision and script-name drift.
evidence: PyPI `kolm` is occupied by an unrelated 2017 package, while local `pyproject.toml` uses that name and exposes the older script name.
why_it_matters: A public Python launch can confuse users or collide with existing ownership.
next_action: Choose a publishable Python name, update scripts/imports, and add CLI-wrapper tests.

claim_or_finding: Homebrew, Windows package-manager, and Docker labels are not channel-backed.
evidence: The implied Homebrew tap, Scoop path, winget path, and likely Docker Hub repo returned 404; local stubs remain preview or incomplete.
why_it_matters: Shipped labels overstate install availability.
next_action: Downgrade labels to source-install recipes until tap, manifests, image, and digest checks pass.

claim_or_finding: The integrations page uses shipped labels where only source code or preview assets exist.
evidence: Local integrations labels GitHub Actions, Node, Python, npm, Homebrew, Windows package managers, and Docker as shipped despite missing package-channel evidence.
why_it_matters: Integration maturity appears stronger than the release system supports.
next_action: Render labels from a release evidence manifest instead of static HTML.

claim_or_finding: No release workflow emits package artifacts or supply-chain proofs.
evidence: Workflow search found no npm publish, PyPI upload, Docker push, VS Code publish, Cosign, SLSA, SBOM, or release-asset publishing steps.
why_it_matters: Supply-chain proof remains roadmap, not release evidence.
next_action: Add a tagged release workflow with artifact, checksum, signature, provenance, and SBOM verification.

claim_or_finding: SDK package metadata still carries stale naming and encoding drift.
evidence: Python README and VS Code package metadata retain older naming, and VS Code metadata shows replacement-character corruption.
why_it_matters: Publishing these packages would revive stale positioning and look unpolished.
next_action: Clean SDK metadata and docs before marketplace or registry publication.

claim_or_finding: Release labels are hand-written instead of generated from channel checks.
evidence: No release evidence manifest was found; docs and integrations encode status labels directly in HTML.
why_it_matters: Manual labels will keep drifting as package state changes.
next_action: Create `release-evidence.json` and generate docs badges from verified channel checks.

claim_or_finding: The strongest competitor wedge is trace import, not another dashboard.
evidence: Langfuse, LangSmith, Braintrust, Helicone, Phoenix, Weave, OpenPipe, LiteLLM, Vercel, Cloudflare, and Portkey all expose trace, dataset, export, callback, middleware, or custom-provider surfaces.
why_it_matters: Kolm can use existing traffic systems as evidence sources instead of competing with them directly.
next_action: Build importers and route middleware around a source-neutral trace schema.

claim_or_finding: Kolm has capture/export primitives but no external `kolm import` layer.
evidence: Local code supports OpenAI/Anthropic capture and labels export, but source search found no external trace import command or competitor-specific connector.
why_it_matters: The best acquisition path from competitor ecosystems is unimplemented.
next_action: Add `kolm import langfuse` and importer fixtures before more observability UI.

claim_or_finding: Langfuse is the highest-leverage first importer.
evidence: Official Langfuse docs expose traces, observations, scores, datasets, metrics, SDK/API queries, and export paths, and Langfuse has self-host/open-source buyer overlap.
why_it_matters: A Langfuse fixture can prove source-compatible trace-to-artifact flow quickly.
next_action: Build a Langfuse query/export fixture into `kolm-trace-1` and compile a recipe-only artifact.

claim_or_finding: Helicone and LiteLLM are fast paths from production requests into Kolm.
evidence: Helicone exposes request/dataset export paths; LiteLLM exposes callbacks and proxy hooks around request and response events.
why_it_matters: These tools already sit in production traffic and can identify repeated tasks.
next_action: Build Helicone JSONL import and LiteLLM callback before generic gateway work.

claim_or_finding: LangSmith, Braintrust, and Weave should feed K-score instead of being cloned.
evidence: Official docs expose trace exports, datasets, experiments, scores, scorer stats, and evaluation results.
why_it_matters: Kolm's differentiator is artifact acceptance and receipts, not another eval dashboard.
next_action: Map external scores and datasets into artifact eval cases and K-score provenance.

claim_or_finding: Vercel AI SDK, Cloudflare AI Gateway, and Portkey can route artifact-first once Kolm exposes a safe target.
evidence: Vercel supports language-model middleware; Cloudflare supports custom providers; Portkey supports private/self-hosted provider routing.
why_it_matters: Gateway route integrations need a stable runtime API and trust policy, not just docs snippets.
next_action: Design artifact-first middleware after sandbox trust tiers and public-run policy are fixed.

claim_or_finding: OpenPipe directly owns logs-to-hosted-replacement positioning.
evidence: OpenPipe docs describe request capture, JSONL export, fine-tuning, evaluation, hosting, caching, and OpenAI-format model replacement.
why_it_matters: Kolm will lose a generic cost-savings comparison unless it proves portability and receipts.
next_action: Compare OpenPipe output against signed portable Kolm artifact proof, not generic fine-tuning.

claim_or_finding: Predibase makes unproved Kolm LoRA claims especially risky.
evidence: Predibase docs show dataset upload, adapter creation, evaluation, deployment, and LoRA serving from production deployments.
why_it_matters: Kolm's model-adapter story is not competitive until artifacts contain inspectable adapter evidence.
next_action: Treat Predibase as a training backend and keep Kolm LoRA copy roadmap until model-adapter artifacts are real.

claim_or_finding: Kolm needs a source-neutral trace/eval schema before building many connectors.
evidence: Competitor sources expose different IDs, dataset versions, inputs, outputs, scores, prompts, costs, latency, users, sessions, tags, and metadata.
why_it_matters: Without a schema, each importer will create incompatible proof and retention behavior.
next_action: Define `kolm-trace-1` with provenance, consent, redaction, score, and source-system fields.

claim_or_finding: Trace imports need redaction, consent, and receipt write-back from day one.
evidence: Local capture stores prompt/response text; no external importer policy or source write-back contract exists.
why_it_matters: Imported traces can contain sensitive data and Kolm results can disappear from the user's existing workflow.
next_action: Add redaction/consent gates and write artifact IDs, K-scores, receipts, and avoided-call metrics back to source systems.

claim_or_finding: `kolm-trace-1` should ship before any external connector.
evidence: The schema pass mapped local observations, `rs-1-evals`, benchmark reports, Langfuse, LangSmith, Braintrust, Helicone, Phoenix, Weave, OpenPipe, and gateway hooks into one row model.
why_it_matters: Without a schema, connectors will silently lose scores, trace hierarchy, tool calls, cost evidence, and privacy state.
next_action: Add JSON schemas and golden fixtures before implementing API importers.

claim_or_finding: Trace import must require an explicit redaction mode.
evidence: The proposed row carries redaction_state, consent_basis, retention_days, data residency, user/session hashes, and raw payload refs; current local capture has no equivalent importer policy.
why_it_matters: Importing production traces without this gate creates compliance and retention risk before product value is proven.
next_action: Make importer commands fail unless a privacy mode is selected and recorded per row.

claim_or_finding: External scores are evidence, not K-score.
evidence: Langfuse, Braintrust, Weave, LangSmith, and other tools expose source scores or feedback, while Kolm K-score must be recomputed from embedded eval cases and artifact runs.
why_it_matters: Treating source scores as K-score would make artifact proof non-reproducible.
next_action: Store scores[] in importer rows, derive eval cases, and recompute K-score independently.

claim_or_finding: Imported rows need checksums and source IDs before receipt write-back.
evidence: The schema defines source_checksum, row_checksum, source trace/span IDs, import receipt IDs, and writeback state; current Kolm receipts only bind artifact/eval hashes.
why_it_matters: Without provenance, Kolm cannot prove which source traces produced an artifact or write credible receipts back to source systems.
next_action: Implement import manifests with row checksums before source-system write-back.

claim_or_finding: Namespace drift will break importer and label consistency.
evidence: Capture rows use `corpus_namespace`, while bridge inbox filters and display paths read `namespace`; labels export has special-case corpus namespace filtering.
why_it_matters: Importer rows need one namespace contract for tenant routing, export, and distill thresholds.
next_action: Normalize legacy capture rows into tenant.namespace and add regression tests.

claim_or_finding: OpenPipe and gateway sources require structured messages, tools, and tool_choice.
evidence: OpenPipe exports messages, tools, tool_choice, and split; Helicone and LangSmith also preserve structured chat data in export surfaces.
why_it_matters: Flattening chat/tool rows into prompt text would lose the behavior Kolm needs to compile or route artifact-first.
next_action: Make input.messages, input.tools, output.message, and output.tool_calls first-class schema fields.

claim_or_finding: LangSmith and Phoenix imports must preserve trace/span hierarchy.
evidence: LangSmith run data includes trace IDs, run IDs, parent IDs, child IDs, run types, dotted order, status, inputs, outputs, feedback, costs, and tokens; Phoenix centers trace import/export and span querying.
why_it_matters: Agent and tool-call behavior cannot be compiled later if hierarchy is flattened away.
next_action: Require source.trace_id, source.span_id, and source.parent_span_id whenever a source provides them.

claim_or_finding: Importer claims should wait for a field-preservation fixture pack.
evidence: The schema spec lists first fixtures for Langfuse, Helicone, OpenPipe, LangSmith, Weave, local observations, and `rs-1-evals`; no such fixtures exist today.
why_it_matters: A connector can appear to work while dropping the fields that prove Kolm value.
next_action: Add valid/invalid fixtures and loss-report tests before public importer claims.

claim_or_finding: Avoided-call proof needs source cost fields joined to benchmark receipts.
evidence: Local benchmark reports prove artifact behavior and egress, but do not join to original source model, token counts, or cost.
why_it_matters: Savings claims remain unsupported unless imported traces carry original cost context into benchmark/report sidecars.
next_action: Add source_trace_ids, original model, original cost, fallback count, and receipt IDs to benchmark sidecars.

claim_or_finding: The first importer should be fixture-only, not live API-first.
evidence: The schema pass found enough source shape divergence that field names and loss rules should be locked with local fixtures before adding API credentials, pagination, Parquet, or webhooks.
why_it_matters: Live API work before schema tests will create brittle connectors and harder privacy review.
next_action: Implement `kolm import langfuse --fixture` style normalization first, then add authenticated API fetchers.

claim_or_finding: External source scores must not become K-score directly.
evidence: Local K-score is computed from artifact replay, size, latency, cost, and coverage; external systems expose their own score types, feedback, scorer origins, directions, and aggregates.
why_it_matters: Letting source scores set K-score would break portable proof and receipt semantics.
next_action: Implement a no-direct-source-score-to-K-score guard in importer tests.

claim_or_finding: Numeric score import needs scale, direction, and threshold.
evidence: Langfuse numeric scores, Braintrust score maps, Weave scorer values, and LangSmith feedback scores can all use different scales and meanings.
why_it_matters: A raw 0.8 score is meaningless without knowing whether higher is better, lower is better, or what threshold applies.
next_action: Require score-map config before numeric scores affect eval selection.

claim_or_finding: Categorical scores require explicit maps.
evidence: Langfuse categorical scores, LangSmith value fields, and Phoenix labels carry project-specific labels; only a few obvious pass/fail labels are safe defaults.
why_it_matters: Unknown labels can silently invert quality signals.
next_action: Fail closed for unknown categorical labels and report them as loss rows.

claim_or_finding: Scorer origin must survive import.
evidence: LangSmith feedback_source and Phoenix evaluator kind distinguish app/user/human/LLM/code signals; local evals currently do not preserve scorer origin.
why_it_matters: A user thumbs-up, a human review, and an LLM judge should not carry the same audit weight.
next_action: Map scorer.kind and allow eval selection policy by scorer origin.

claim_or_finding: Aggregate score summaries are manifest evidence, not eval rows.
evidence: Weave summaries, LangSmith feedback_stats, and analytics-style score summaries cannot reconstruct per-row expected outputs or pass/fail labels.
why_it_matters: Using aggregates as eval cases would fabricate artifact proof.
next_action: Store aggregate scores only in importer manifests and block eval-case generation from them.

claim_or_finding: Kolm currently lacks rubric and partial-credit replay.
evidence: Artifact eval replay uses deep equality against expected output; external systems support comments, explanations, labels, and judge rubrics.
why_it_matters: Imported rubric evidence will not be reproduced by K-score until a rubric runner exists.
next_action: Keep rubric text as evidence first, then add a separate rubric evaluator before public rubric claims.

claim_or_finding: Weave row digests are valuable for source dedupe and reproducibility.
evidence: Weave eval export returns row digests, resolved dataset inputs, model outputs, scores, and scorer summaries.
why_it_matters: Kolm needs content-addressable row IDs to prevent duplicate imported eval rows from inflating coverage.
next_action: Use row_digest as source checksum or source row ID when available.

claim_or_finding: Braintrust score maps need per-project normalization.
evidence: BTQL can query logs, experiments, datasets, and score fields, but score keys and scales are project-defined.
why_it_matters: A generic Braintrust importer could preserve data while misusing score semantics.
next_action: Require a project score-map and emit loss reports for unmapped score keys.

claim_or_finding: Train/test/holdout split policy must be independent of source score value.
evidence: Helicone curation and OpenPipe split fields are selection/routing metadata, not quality scores; source scores can help trust rows but should not erase split boundaries.
why_it_matters: Savings and K-score claims fail if train rows leak into holdout evals.
next_action: Honor source split and require explicit eval_use before scores select holdouts.

claim_or_finding: Score normalization needs negative fixtures before live importer APIs.
evidence: The matrix identifies missing direction, unknown categorical values, aggregate-only scores, conflicting scorer origins, text comments, and direct-to-K-score risks.
why_it_matters: Importer tests that only cover happy paths will ship misleading score behavior.
next_action: Add valid/invalid normalization fixtures and loss-report assertions before API fetchers.

claim_or_finding: Trace import should fail closed without an explicit privacy mode.
evidence: The privacy policy defines hash-only, redacted, raw, and blocked modes; current local capture stores raw prompt/response text without retention or redaction controls.
why_it_matters: External importers can multiply the existing capture privacy gap unless mode and retention are mandatory.
next_action: Require `--privacy`, `--retention-days`, and namespace for importer commands, with raw mode requiring an additional allow flag.

claim_or_finding: Imported traces need row-level retention and purge metadata.
evidence: Langfuse, LangSmith, and Phoenix all expose retention/deletion controls, while Kolm has no import retention job or purge manifest.
why_it_matters: Buyers will expect imported data to honor explicit lifecycle controls at least as clearly as source systems do.
next_action: Add purge_after, retention_days, and import manifest tracking to every imported row.

claim_or_finding: Raw payloads should live in sidecars, not normalized rows or artifacts.
evidence: The privacy policy separates raw_payload_ref/checksum from normalized fields; current local capture stores prompt/response inline in observations.
why_it_matters: Inline raw data makes purge, redaction, and artifact boundaries brittle.
next_action: Implement raw sidecar storage with checksums and purge tests before raw import.

claim_or_finding: Portable artifacts must not contain source privacy metadata.
evidence: The privacy matrix blocks raw source trace IDs, user/session IDs, raw refs, comments, delete refs, and loss reports from `.kolm` eval embedding.
why_it_matters: A distributed artifact cannot be reliably purged after a source deletion request.
next_action: Keep source provenance in manifests and receipts, and embed only approved eval fields.

claim_or_finding: Import purge must be implemented before deletion claims.
evidence: Langfuse deletes related scores/observations with traces; LangSmith deletes related feedback/aggregations/stats; Braintrust exposes delete-by-row API patterns; Kolm has no import purge command.
why_it_matters: A source deletion or customer account delete will not remove imported local rows today.
next_action: Specify and implement `kolm import purge` over rows, sidecars, loss reports, labels, cache, jobs, and write-back state.

claim_or_finding: Redacted import needs detector reports and no-sample loss reports.
evidence: The proposed redacted mode stores redacted IO plus original checksums and detector paths/counts; loss reports should not include raw sample values by default.
why_it_matters: Debug and loss reports can become ungoverned shadow data stores.
next_action: Add redaction report and no-sample loss-report fixtures.

claim_or_finding: Source user and session IDs should be tenant-salted hashes.
evidence: The privacy policy requires user_hash and session_hash with tenant-scoped salt instead of direct source IDs.
why_it_matters: Direct identifiers can leak into manifests, dashboards, logs, or artifacts.
next_action: Add identity hashing and no-direct-ID fixtures.

claim_or_finding: Current capture must migrate to importer privacy semantics.
evidence: Capture has raw prompt/response storage, namespace drift, non-binding discard controls, and no retention/purge route.
why_it_matters: External importer privacy would be inconsistent if first-party capture remains weaker.
next_action: Apply the same privacy fields, kept/discarded semantics, and retention to capture rows.

claim_or_finding: Phoenix-style self-hosted and air-gapped users need offline fixture import.
evidence: Phoenix self-hosted privacy keeps application data in customer infrastructure and supports telemetry/external-resource controls; Kolm importers should not require live API calls or remote services for fixture conversion.
why_it_matters: A hosted-only importer would weaken the private/offline buyer story.
next_action: Keep first importer fixture-only and fully offline; add API fetchers later.

claim_or_finding: Importer privacy tests should block public importer claims.
evidence: The policy identifies missing retention, raw allow flags, blocked modes, identity hashing, sidecar purge, artifact allowlist, and no-sample loss reports as required tests.
why_it_matters: Without negative privacy tests, importer claims can ship while lifecycle behavior is false.
next_action: Make importer privacy fixtures a launch-blocking CI gate.

claim_or_finding: A durable import manifest must ship before any importer.
evidence: The manifest contract defines source checksum, privacy mode, retention, row refs, counts, artifacts, loss reports, and purge state; no local equivalent exists today.
why_it_matters: Without a manifest, Kolm cannot prove what was imported or purge it reliably.
next_action: Add `kolm-import-manifest-1` schema and fixture writer before source-specific importers.

claim_or_finding: `kolm import purge` needs dry-run before destructive modes.
evidence: The contract defines dry-run targets for rows, scores, raw sidecars, labels, cache keys, writebacks, non-purgeable artifacts, and blocked targets.
why_it_matters: Operators need exact impact before deleting customer data.
next_action: Implement purge dry-run and require manifest_id confirmation for delete/anonymize.

claim_or_finding: Row-level purge is feasible in the current store facade but not wired to import rows.
evidence: `src/store.js` exposes JSON and SQLite remove/update primitives with SQLite transactions; import_rows/import_scores/import_manifests do not exist.
why_it_matters: The infrastructure can support purge if new rows carry manifest_id.
next_action: Add manifest_id to future import rows and cover JSON/SQLite parity.

claim_or_finding: Cache purge is currently incomplete.
evidence: `src/cache.js` can invalidate by version_id but cannot delete by import row/cache key and writes under data/cache outside the configured lifecycle noted in prior audits.
why_it_matters: Imported sensitive outputs can survive row purge in disk cache.
next_action: Add cache delete-by-key and align cache root with configured data lifecycle.

claim_or_finding: Purge evidence cannot depend on source rows that purge deletes.
evidence: The current audit endpoint reconstructs events from invocations, compile_jobs, observations, and stripe_events; deleting imported rows would erase reconstructed evidence.
why_it_matters: A customer needs proof that purge ran even after rows are gone.
next_action: Add durable purge audit events or signed manifest status updates.

claim_or_finding: Delete and anonymize need separate contracts.
evidence: The purge contract defines delete as target removal and anonymize as keeping counters/checksums while removing IO, identities, comments, and raw refs.
why_it_matters: Some buyers need audit totals without retaining sensitive content.
next_action: Add anonymize patch allowlist and fixtures.

claim_or_finding: Distributed artifacts are non-purgeable outputs.
evidence: The purge contract explicitly marks downloaded/published `.kolm` artifacts as non-purgeable and relies on keeping source privacy metadata out of artifacts.
why_it_matters: Promising artifact deletion would be false once artifacts leave Kolm control.
next_action: Report artifact refs as non-purgeable in purge dry-run and final reports.

claim_or_finding: Loss reports must be first-class purge targets.
evidence: Import loss reports are required for field-loss transparency but can become shadow stores of sensitive paths or samples.
why_it_matters: Deleting rows while keeping loss reports can violate the same privacy intent.
next_action: Track loss_report_ref in manifests and purge/anonymize it with rows.

claim_or_finding: Purge should be idempotent and partial-failure aware.
evidence: The matrix calls for manifest status, already_purged responses, per-target results, and partial_failed state.
why_it_matters: Retrying a failed purge should not corrupt counts or hide undeleted sidecars.
next_action: Design purge around manifest state transitions and per-target result logs.

claim_or_finding: Manifest and purge fixtures should block importer launch.
evidence: No manifest or purge fixtures exist; the matrix requires valid/invalid manifest, dry-run, delete, anonymize, partial failure, cache, and artifact-boundary coverage.
why_it_matters: Importer lifecycle claims will be untestable without these fixtures.
next_action: Make manifest/purge tests launch blockers for importer docs and APIs.

claim_or_finding: The first source-specific importer should be fixture-only.
evidence: Langfuse has traces observations scores datasets retention and deletion surfaces that cover the full import contract, but Kolm has no import command yet.
why_it_matters: Live API work before fixtures can hide schema privacy and loss-report defects behind credentials.
next_action: Ship an offline Langfuse fixture importer contract before any live connector.

claim_or_finding: Existing capture and labels cannot substitute for Langfuse import.
evidence: Local capture flattens prompt and response rows, lacks source hierarchy scores dataset links retention and manifest state, and has namespace drift.
why_it_matters: Reusing capture storage directly would make imported evidence lossy and hard to purge.
next_action: Emit normalized JSONL manifest and loss reports first, then decide storage.

claim_or_finding: Langfuse observation field groups need structural preservation.
evidence: The Observations API exposes IO metadata model usage prompt metrics latency and cost fields through field groups and cursor pagination.
why_it_matters: Partial field selection can silently drop the fields Kolm needs for savings and eval proof.
next_action: Add full and minimal field-group fixtures with cursor-completion checks.

claim_or_finding: Langfuse scores must stay type-preserving and fail closed for eval use.
evidence: Langfuse score types include numeric categorical boolean and text values attached to traces observations sessions or dataset runs.
why_it_matters: Copying source scores into K-score would corrupt Kolm conformance semantics.
next_action: Map scores into `kolm-score-1` and require score-map rules for any eval-selection use.

claim_or_finding: Langfuse dataset source links should stay outside artifact eval bodies.
evidence: Dataset items can link back to source traces and observations, but portable eval cases should not embed production source IDs.
why_it_matters: Distributed artifacts can outlive purge controls and expose source identifiers.
next_action: Keep source trace and observation IDs in manifests or sidecars only.

claim_or_finding: Dataset versions retention and deletion need manifest windows and purge refs.
evidence: Langfuse datasets are versioned by timestamp and Langfuse documents retention and deletion behavior for source data.
why_it_matters: Imported eval packs can become non-reproducible or lifecycle-inconsistent without window and purge metadata.
next_action: Record dataset version timestamp retention_days purge_after and source deletion refs in the manifest.

claim_or_finding: Langfuse privacy modes must change output deterministically.
evidence: The importer privacy policy defines hash-only redacted raw and blocked modes, and the Langfuse fixture matrix requires mode-specific expected outputs.
why_it_matters: Privacy regressions are easy to miss if only normalized happy-path rows are tested.
next_action: Add redacted and hash-only snapshots before raw mode support.

claim_or_finding: Langfuse pagination and field groups are API-fetch blockers.
evidence: Langfuse query surfaces include cursor pagination and selectable observation field groups that affect what data is available.
why_it_matters: A live connector can silently import incomplete traces if pagination or field groups are wrong.
next_action: Make fixture pagination and field-group preservation gates before API fetch support.

claim_or_finding: The Langfuse importer must never emit K-score directly.
evidence: The score normalization policy requires external source scores to remain evidence while Kolm recomputes K-score from artifact evals and receipts.
why_it_matters: External score reuse would make Kolm's proof metric source-dependent and non-portable.
next_action: Add fixture validation that fails on k_score eval_score or artifact score fields in imported rows.

claim_or_finding: The Langfuse fixture pack is the next importer launch blocker.
evidence: The current research now defines trace schema score normalization privacy manifest purge and a Langfuse-specific fixture matrix, but no fixture files or importer exist.
why_it_matters: Shipping importer claims without this pack would repeat the broader claim-vs-proof gap.
next_action: Build the Langfuse fixture pack and validation harness before other source importers or write-back work.

claim_or_finding: The first Langfuse fixture must be page-based, not a merged export.
evidence: Observations API v2 uses cursor pagination and field groups; the blueprint defines two observation pages with a final null cursor.
why_it_matters: A merged fixture cannot catch incomplete cursor consumption or missing field groups.
next_action: Materialize page fixtures and assert cursor exhaustion.

claim_or_finding: Redacted and hash-only snapshots should precede raw mode.
evidence: The blueprint defines redacted normalized/eval/manifest/loss outputs plus hash-only normalized/loss outputs and intentionally defers raw-mode snapshots.
why_it_matters: Raw fixtures can normalize unsafe sample retention before safer modes are locked.
next_action: Implement redacted mode first and hash-only mode second.

claim_or_finding: The Langfuse fixture needs both mapped and unmapped score cases.
evidence: The score-map blueprint maps numeric correctness, boolean resolved, and categorical risk_label while leaving tone_label unmapped.
why_it_matters: Only happy-path score fixtures would miss fail-closed behavior.
next_action: Add score.categorical_map_missing as a required loss class.

claim_or_finding: Dataset source links must be denied from expected evalcases.
evidence: Langfuse dataset items can include sourceTraceId and sourceObservationId, but the expected evalcase contract excludes those fields.
why_it_matters: Artifact evals can leave the purge boundary and leak production trace identifiers.
next_action: Enforce an evalcase source-ID denylist in the fixture harness.

claim_or_finding: Observation field-group completeness is a fixture requirement.
evidence: The blueprint requires IO metadata model usage prompt and metrics fields because default Langfuse v2 field groups return only core and basic.
why_it_matters: Default API responses would omit cost latency and prompt proof data.
next_action: Require explicit field-group assertions before live API fetch.

claim_or_finding: The fixture pack needs named loss classes before implementation.
evidence: The blueprint names parse failure non-generation skip score-map miss archived skip and missing expected-output losses.
why_it_matters: Silent drops would make import completeness and customer review impossible.
next_action: Treat explicit loss rows as acceptance criteria, not optional diagnostics.

claim_or_finding: Symbolic fixture checksums must be replaced before executable tests ship.
evidence: The blueprint shows shape examples and calls out that final fixtures must compute real canonical source and row checksums.
why_it_matters: Placeholder checksums would make manifest proof cosmetic.
next_action: Add canonical JSON checksum rules and compute fixture checksums in the harness.

claim_or_finding: Langfuse purge dry-run should follow manifest validation, not precede it.
evidence: The fixture file matrix marks expected.purge-dry-run.redacted.json as future until manifest checks pass.
why_it_matters: Testing delete or anonymize before manifest counts are trusted can hide lifecycle bugs.
next_action: Add dry-run fixture only after manifest parity validation passes.

claim_or_finding: The fixture harness must be semantic, not just snapshot-based.
evidence: The blueprint requires pagination, field groups, structured IO, score typing, loss rows, denylist, mode comparison, no-K-score, and manifest count assertions.
why_it_matters: Golden snapshots alone can accept wrong behavior if field names or privacy modes drift.
next_action: Write semantic assertions around every fixture invariant.

claim_or_finding: Live Langfuse API support remains blocked until fixture parity passes.
evidence: The backlog now requires fixture parity, pagination coverage, field-group preservation, privacy modes, and purge dry-run before API fetch support.
why_it_matters: A live connector without fixtures would be hard to test without credentials and could import sensitive data incorrectly.
next_action: Make the executable fixture pack the merge gate for live Langfuse connector work.

claim_or_finding: Import rows need domain-separated canonical checksums.
evidence: The checksum memo defines JCS-plus-SHA-256 envelopes for source rows, normalized rows, score rows, evalcases, loss rows, manifests, JSONL files, and raw sidecars.
why_it_matters: Bare row hashes can collide across roles and cannot explain what was signed or verified.
next_action: Implement checksum envelopes before executable fixture snapshots.

claim_or_finding: RFC 8785 should anchor JSON row checksums.
evidence: RFC 8785 defines deterministic JSON canonicalization with object property sorting, UTF-8 output, primitive serialization, and I-JSON constraints.
why_it_matters: Runtime-specific JSON serialization can produce different hashes for the same semantic object.
next_action: Adopt a tested JCS helper and block importer fixtures on RFC vectors.

claim_or_finding: Source file checksums and source row checksums answer different questions.
evidence: The memo uses byte checksums for exact fixture/export files and JCS row checksums for parsed source rows.
why_it_matters: A semantic row hash cannot prove exact exported file provenance and a byte hash cannot prove row-level mapping.
next_action: Store both file and row checksums in manifests.

claim_or_finding: Privacy mode must be inside normalized checksum envelopes.
evidence: Redacted and hash-only outputs can share source rows but have different payloads and different privacy obligations.
why_it_matters: Mode collapse can hide privacy regressions while checksums still appear valid.
next_action: Include privacy_mode in normalized score evalcase and loss checksum envelopes.

claim_or_finding: Manifest content and purge state need separate checksums.
evidence: The memo separates immutable import facts from mutable purge/anonymize state through manifest_content_checksum and manifest_state_checksum.
why_it_matters: Updating purge state should not rewrite the original import proof.
next_action: Dual-hash manifests and verify state transitions independently.

claim_or_finding: Loss row checksums must not depend on raw samples by default.
evidence: Loss rows include code path count privacy mode and sample policy, while raw samples are excluded outside explicit raw mode.
why_it_matters: Loss reports can leak sensitive source payloads and become unpurged shadow stores.
next_action: Keep redacted and hash-only loss reports sample-free and separately checksum raw sidecars only in raw mode.

claim_or_finding: Expected JSONL files need canonical line order.
evidence: The memo requires one JCS object per line, stable row_id ordering, LF joining, and explicit trailing-LF handling for file checksums.
why_it_matters: Golden fixtures can change for irrelevant formatting reasons or hide row omissions.
next_action: Make JSONL order and trailing-LF policy part of the fixture harness.

claim_or_finding: Import checksums should reject rounding and non-finite numbers.
evidence: The checksum rules keep source numeric values as parsed, convert normalized latency to integer microseconds, and reject NaN or Infinity.
why_it_matters: Cost latency and score evidence can drift if helpers round or stringify numbers differently.
next_action: Add numeric test vectors for Langfuse cost latency and score fields.

claim_or_finding: Symbolic checksum placeholders must not reach executable fixtures.
evidence: The Langfuse blueprint intentionally uses symbolic checksum examples and the checksum matrix requires rejection of placeholder patterns.
why_it_matters: Cosmetic checksums would give false confidence in manifest integrity.
next_action: Replace placeholders with computed JCS and byte hashes before fixture tests become launch gates.

claim_or_finding: Checksum test vectors should block live importer work.
evidence: The checksum matrix requires RFC vectors, domain envelope tests, source byte checksums, privacy-mode differences, and manifest state checks before product importers.
why_it_matters: Live connectors without stable checksum rules cannot produce durable audit evidence.
next_action: Make checksum harness completion a prerequisite for Langfuse fixture generation and live API fetch.

claim_or_finding: Kolm should position as a compiler cache for intelligence, not as another on-device runtime.
evidence: Apple Core ML, Google LiteRT, ONNX Runtime Mobile, ExecuTorch, llama.cpp, and MLC all provide strong free or bundled execution paths.
why_it_matters: Raw local inference is being commoditized; the fundable product is cross-runtime compile, eval, receipt, registry, and governance.
next_action: Rewrite the venture narrative and public research page around artifact evidence above commodity runtimes.

claim_or_finding: Native runtime baselines are mandatory before broad cross-platform claims expand.
evidence: Core ML, LiteRT, ONNX Runtime, ExecuTorch, llama.cpp, and MLC official docs all expose mature deployment paths or local inference capabilities.
why_it_matters: Developers and investors will not accept generic performance claims against free native tooling.
next_action: Publish a benchmark pack with p50 p95 artifact size memory energy proxy fallback and K-score by target.

claim_or_finding: Local personalization is the highest-leverage claim and the least specified one.
evidence: The redline memo requires a method decision across retrieval calibration adapter training or another mechanism plus storage deletion and hardware limits.
why_it_matters: If personalization is vague it reads as aspirational and creates product, privacy, and app-review risk.
next_action: Write the personalization mechanics spec before featuring the claim more aggressively.

claim_or_finding: The registry should be the recurring moat rather than an afterthought.
evidence: A reviewed catalog can carry K-score history, device targets, provenance, receipts, revocation, and private namespaces.
why_it_matters: Compile-time SaaS pricing is thin unless artifact governance and registry retention create recurring value.
next_action: Build public artifact detail pages and private namespace controls around trust evidence.

claim_or_finding: Compliance positioning must become an evidence map instead of broad public claims.
evidence: EU AI Act and HHS Security Rule sources create demand for documented controls but do not make any tool automatically sufficient.
why_it_matters: Regulated buyers will ask for BAA DPA subprocessors retention audit logs support access limitations and review dates.
next_action: Create a compliance evidence pack with owner review date limitation and customer action fields.
